Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS FOR NON-COMPLIANCE OF COURT ORDER

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: granted in small part. No later than May 8, 2026 cross-defendant Electric Power Systems, International Inc. must pay $5,000 in reasonable monetary sanctions for its pattern of discovery abuse by failing to comply with the order filed March 13, 2026. These sanctions are authorized by CCP 2023.030 per City of Los Angeles v. PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP (2024) 17 Cal. 5th 46, 74-75. KES' request for $60,000 is excessive in two ways. First, it appears to be based in part on costs it claimed to have incurred to obtain the order filed March 13, 2026, yet the order resolved the issue of KES' entitlement to costs to obtain that order. Second, while EPS' pattern of discovery abuse is troubling, there is an insufficient showing that the amount KES seeks is commensurate with the harm it has suffered due to EPS' discovery abuse since the issuance of the March 13, 2026 order or that the amount sought is necessary to induce EPS to comply with its discovery obligations in the future. No opposition filed. Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)

Counsel for Cross-Defendant Electrical Power Systems International, Inc., subject of today's motion, informed the Court by email received on Saturday, March 7, 2026, that he is currently in trial in Department 504 and is unable to appear at today's hearing. Court acknowledges receipt of Cross-Defendant's notice and advises the parties present that it is appropriate to proceed because the motion was properly noticed. The cross-defendant's late request for a continuance does not negate the effect of proper notice.

Matter is discussed. Not Argued. Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KAPOLEI ENERGY STORAGE I, LLC'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Electric Power Systems International, Inc. And Compel Attendance At Person Most Qualified Deposition

The unopposed motion by Kapolei Energy Storage I, LLC (KES) to compel production of documents and attendance at PMQ deposition by Electric Power Systems International, Inc. (EPS), is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. EPS's shall produce documents in accord with its statements of compliance by March 23, 2026. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.320.) EPS shall meet and confer forthwith and by no later than March 16, 2026 about a date for the deposition of its PMQ and shall identify its PMQ.

KES seeks sanctions. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2023.010, subd. (d); id., sec. 2023.020; id., sec. 2031.320, subd. (b).) KES' motion does not include a declaration "setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought" (id., sec. 2023.040), and KES does not request a particular amount. The court concludes that EPS's failure to submit to authorized methods of discovery is a misuse of the discovery process unsupported by substantial justification and orders EPS to pay $1000 in sanctions to KES within 30 days.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
